content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to order food from a food delivery app. He wishes to order once today, and buy three items costing A1,A2A_1, A_2A1​,A2​ and A3A_3A3​ rupees, respectively. He'll also order once tomorrow, when he'll buy three items costing B1,B2B_1, B_2B1​,B2​ and B3B_3B3​ rupees, respectively. There is an additional delivery charge of rupees DDD for each order.

He notices that there is a coupon on sale, which costs rupees CCC. If he buys that coupon, the delivery charges on any day, on an order of rupees 150150150 or more shall be waived (that is, the DDD rupees will not be added, if the sum of the costs of the items is ≥150\ge 150≥150).

Note that Chef is ordering the three items together on each day, so the delivery charge is charged only once each day. Also, note that it's only needed to buy the coupon once to avail the delivery fee waiver on both days.

Should Chef buy the coupon? Note that Chef shall buy the coupon only if it costs him **strictly less** than what it costs him without the coupon, in total.

###Input:


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers DDD and CCC, denoting the delivery charge, and the price of the coupon, respectively.
The second line of each test case contains three space-separated integers A1,A2A_1, A_2A1​,A2​ and A3A_3A3​, denoting the prices of the food items that Chef plans to order on Day 111, respectively.
The third line of each test case contains three space-separated integers B1B_1B1​, B2B_2B2​ and B3B_3B3​, denoting the prices of the food items that Chef plans to order on Day 222, respectively.
###Output:
For each test case, output **YES** if Chef should buy the coupon, and **NO** otherwise, in a separate line.

###Constraints


1≤T≤1041 \le T \le 10^41≤T≤104
1≤D,C≤1001 \le D,C \le 1001≤D,C≤100
1≤A1,A2,A3≤1001 \le  A_1, A_2, A_3  \le 1001≤A1​,A2​,A3​≤100
1≤B1,B2,B3≤1001 \le B_1, B_2, B_3 \le 1001≤B1​,B2​,B3​≤100
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains two integers D and C – the delivery charge and the coupon price. Second line contains three integers A1 A2 A3 – prices of the three items ordered on day 1. Third line contains three integers B1 B2 B3 – prices of the three items ordered on day 2.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef should buy the coupon (i.e., total cost with the coupon is strictly less than without it), otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: sumA=160>=150, sumB=240>=150. Without coupon cost=160+240+2*90=580. With coupon cost=160+240+100=500. 500<580 => YES.\nTest case 2: sumA=300>=150, sumB=60<150. Without coupon cost=300+60+2*30=420. With coupon cost=300+60+30+30=420. Not strictly less, so NO.
sumA=300>=150, sumB=60<150. Without coupon cost=300+60+2*30=420. With coupon cost=300+60+45+30=435. 435>420, so NO.
sumA=140<150, sumB=160>=150. Without coupon cost=140+160+2*10=320. With coupon cost=140+160+5+10=315. 315<320, so YES.

solution.approach: For each test case compute sumA = A1+A2+A3 and sumB = B1+B2+B3. Without the coupon the total cost is sumA + sumB + 2*D (delivery charge each day). With the coupon the total cost is sumA + sumB + C plus a delivery charge D for each day whose sum is strictly less than 150 (the coupon waives delivery only when the day's total is >=150). Compare the two totals; if the cost with the coupon is strictly smaller, answer YES, otherwise NO.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        D = int(next(it)); C = int(next(it))
        a1 = int(next(it)); a2 = int(next(it)); a3 = int(next(it))
        b1 = int(next(it)); b2 = int(next(it)); b3 = int(next(it))
        sumA = a1 + a2 + a3
        sumB = b1 + b2 + b3
        cost_without = sumA + sumB + 2 * D
        extra = C
        if sumA < 150:
            extra += D
        if sumB < 150:
            extra += D
        cost_with = sumA + sumB + extra
        out.append('YES' if cost_with < cost_without else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
